REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT JOHN ABELSON, CHRISTINE GUTHRIE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO CARRY ITS BURDEN AND SHOW THAT THERE ARE NO TRIABLE ISSUES OF MATERIAL FACT. E.G. ONE OF THE MATERIAL FACTS INCLUDED IN THE SEPARATE STATEMENT FOR MSJ AND EACH ISSUE FOR MSA IS: "PLAINTIFF BEGAN HER TENANCY AT THE SUBJECT PROPERTY IN 2000," DEFENDANT APPEARS TO BE DISPUTING THE EXISTENCE OF TENANCY. SEE RESPONSE TO PLAINTIFF'S SEPARATE STATEMENT, "DEFENDANTS DENY ANY IMPLICATION SUGGESTING PLAINTIFF WAS A TENANT." A LANDLORD-TENANT RELATIONSHIP IS ASSERTED AT THE BASIS OF EACH CAUSE OF ACTION IN THIS ACTION AND THE EXISTENCE OF SUCH RELATIONSHIPS IS BOTH (1) INCLUDED AS A MATERIAL FACT BY THE LITIGANTS; AND (2) DISPUTED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel (1) Inspection Of Defendants Premises, And (2) Defendants Responses To Plaintiffs Supplemental Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Dec-20-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Camilo Artiga-Purcell, Esq. (415.226.7651; ap@artigapurcell.com) for Plaintiff.
Dani Mohling, Esq. (415.981.5411) of Bledsoe, Diestel, Treppa & Crane LLP for Defendant.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF JODI HILLMAN's Motion To Compel (1) Inspection Of Defendants Premises, And (2) Defendants Responses To Plaintiffs Supplemental Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Documents:

Motion denied. On the current record, Plaintiff has failed to establish that a site inspection of the premises 15 years after Plaintiff vacated and the premises were renovated would develop relevant information for trial. Moreover, Defendant has produced an expert declaration stating that an inspection of the premises 15 years after Plaintiff vacated would not show mold or potential exposure at the time Plaintiff resided there. In addition, it appears that a renter is in control of the premises. The motion is denied without prejudice to Plaintiff re-filing with expert declarations establishing the need for the inspection. The motion regarding the supplemental responses is granted. Defendant shall provide supplemental responses and produce responsive documents by December 30, 2021.

Prevailing party to send proposed order tracking the adopted ruling of the court and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to davididaho14@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jul-01-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Joseph Diestel, 415.981.5411 of Bledsoe, Diestel, Treppa,& Crane LLP for Defendant John Abelson.
Camilo Artiga-Purcell, 415.226.7651 of Artiga-Purcell for Plaintiff Jodi Hillman.

Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT JOHN ABELSON, CHRISTINE GUTHRIE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Moving party failed to carry its burden and show that there are no triable issues of material fact. E.g. one of the material facts included in the separate statement for MSJ and each issue for MSA is: "Plaintiff began her tenancy at the subject property in 2000," defendant appears to be disputing the existence of tenancy. See response to Plaintiff's Separate Statement, "Defendants deny any implication suggesting Plaintiff was a tenant." A landlord-tenant relationship is asserted at the basis of each cause of action in this action and the existence of such relationships is both (1) included as a material fact by the litigants; and (2) disputed. Order signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)
